name: IxDict

unions.name: IxDict
unions.comment:
A dictionary of keys and values wrapped with a keyFn `(v -> k)` to derive
keys from that dictionary.

values.name: diff
values.comment: Keep a key-value pair when its key does not appear in the second indexed dictionary.
values.type: IxDict.IxDict comparable v -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v

values.name: empty
values.comment: Create an empty indexed dictionary using a keyFn
values.type: (v -> comparable) -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v

values.name: emptyFromId
values.comment: Uses .id as keyFn
values.type: IxDict.IxDict comparable { a | id : comparable }

values.name: emptyFromTuple
values.comment: Uses Tuple.first as keyFn
values.type: IxDict.IxDict comparable ( comparable, b )

values.name: filter
values.comment:
Filter... use `keep` or `reject` instead.

Because, what does it filter? Do you want the filtered product like coffee
(in this case, use reject) or the remaining good part like gold pannig (in this
case, use keep)
values.type: Basics.Never -> a

values.name: foldl
values.comment: Fold over the key-value pairs in an indexed dictionary from lowest key to highest key.
values.type: (k -> v -> b -> b) -> b -> IxDict.IxDict k v -> b

values.name: foldr
values.comment: Fold over the key-value pairs in an indexed dictionary from highest key to lowest key.
values.type: (k -> v -> b -> b) -> b -> IxDict.IxDict k v -> b

values.name: fromDictBy
values.comment:
Create an indexed dictionary from a normal dictionary by providing a key fn.
Esentially gets the dict values and uses fromListBy:

    fromDictBy : (v -> comparable) -> Dict k v -> IxDict comparable v
    fromDictBy keyFn =
        fromListBy keyFn << Dict.values

See `fromListBy` on how to use it.
values.type: (v -> comparable) -> Dict.Dict k v -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v

values.name: fromListBy
values.comment:
Create an indexed dictionary from list by providing a key fn.

    users : IxDict.IxDict String User
    users =
        IxDict.fromListBy .id
            [ User "alice" "Alice"
            , User "bob" "Bob"
            ]

    type alias User =
        { id : String, name : String }

    updated : IxDict.IxDict String User
    updated =
        users
            |> IxDict.insert (User "charlie" "Charlie")
            |> IxDict.remove "bob"
values.type: (v -> comparable) -> List.List v -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v

values.name: get
values.comment: Get the value associated with a key.
values.type: comparable -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v -> Maybe.Maybe v

values.name: insert
values.comment:
Insert a value into an indexed dictionary. Replaces value when there is
a collision.
values.type: v -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v

values.name: intersect
values.comment: Keep a key-value pair when its key appears in the second indexed dictionary. Preference is given to values in the first indexed dictionary.
values.type: IxDict.IxDict comparable v -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v

values.name: isEmpty
values.comment: Determine if a dictionary is empty.
values.type: IxDict.IxDict k v -> Basics.Bool

values.name: keep
values.comment: Keep only the key-value pairs that pass the given test.
values.type: (comparable -> v -> Basics.Bool) -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v

values.name: keys
values.comment: Get all of the keys in a dictionary, sorted from lowest to highest.
values.type: IxDict.IxDict k v -> List.List k

values.name: map
values.comment:
Map a function onto a ixDict, creating a new ixDict with no duplicates.

This operation is not structure-preserving for sets, so is not a valid Functor. An example case: mapping const x over a ixDict with n > 0 elements will result in a ixDict with one element.
values.type: (comparable -> b -> b) -> IxDict.IxDict comparable b -> IxDict.IxDict comparable b

values.name: mapWith
values.comment: Transform values and rebuild the dict with a new keyFn
values.type: (b -> comparable) -> (comparable -> a -> b) -> IxDict.IxDict comparable a -> IxDict.IxDict comparable b

values.name: member
values.comment: Determine if a key is in a dictionary.
values.type: comparable -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v -> Basics.Bool

values.name: memberByIx
values.comment: Determine if a value is in a dictionary, only by comparing keys.
values.type: v -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v -> Basics.Bool

values.name: memberExact
values.comment: Determine if a value is structually in a dictionary.
values.type: v -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v -> Basics.Bool

values.name: partition
values.comment: Partition an indexed dictionary according to some test. The first dictionary contains all key-value pairs which passed the test, and the second contains the pairs that did not.
values.type: (comparable -> v -> Basics.Bool) -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v -> ( IxDict.IxDict comparable v, IxDict.IxDict comparable v )

values.name: reject
values.comment: Reject all key-value pairs that for a given test
values.type: (comparable -> v -> Basics.Bool) -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v

values.name: remove
values.comment: Remove a key-value pair from the dictionary. If the key is not found, no changes are made.
values.type: comparable -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v

values.name: singleton
values.comment: Create a dictionary with one key-value pair and a keyFn
values.type: (v -> comparable) -> v -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v

values.name: singletonFromId
values.comment: Uses .id as keyFn
values.type: { a | id : comparable } -> IxDict.IxDict comparable { a | id : comparable }

values.name: singletonFromTuple
values.comment: Uses Tuple.first as keyFn
values.type: ( comparable, b ) -> IxDict.IxDict comparable ( comparable, b )

values.name: size
values.comment: Determine the number of key-value pairs in the dictionary.
values.type: IxDict.IxDict k v -> Basics.Int

values.name: toDict
values.comment: Convert an indexed dictionary into a normal dictionary
values.type: IxDict.IxDict k v -> Dict.Dict k v

values.name: toList
values.comment: Convert a dictionary into an association list of key-value pairs, sorted by keys.
values.type: IxDict.IxDict k v -> List.List ( k, v )

values.name: union
values.comment: Combine two indexed dictionaries. If there is a collision, preference is given to the first indexed dictionary.
values.type: IxDict.IxDict comparable v -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v -> IxDict.IxDict comparable v

values.name: values
values.comment: Get all of the values in a dictionary, in the order of their keys.
values.type: IxDict.IxDict k v -> List.List v
